title: TypeScript SDK And Publishing Implementation Tasks
principles: Keep the SDK product-shaped and jurisdiction-neutral at the root.
Preserve strict compile-time relationships between modules, calculations, inputs, outputs, jurisdictions and tax years.
Reuse @whattax/calculators for runtime execution instead of duplicating calculator service behavior.
Expose Effect-native APIs only from explicit Effect subpaths.
Keep public docs neutral about private downstream consumers.

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit after each coherent package-facing slice passes mandatory verification and parent review.
requiredBeforeFinalPR: downstream workspace validation evidence before publication
evidenceRequired: Changed files and package ownership summary
Changeset path and release-train impact, or explicit no-changeset rationale
Type-test evidence for compile-time safety guarantees
Runtime parity evidence against @whattax/calculators and HTTP API where relevant, with HTTP API depending on SDK rather than SDK depending on HTTP API
Import-boundary evidence proving SDK has no @whattax/http-api dependency and browser-safe entrypoints avoid server-only imports
Downstream workspace validation evidence for release-prep tasks

tasks.title: Scaffold the SDK package and export map
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.

Before editing:

- Read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in `docs/architecture/*`.
- Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.

Implementation rules:

- MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: `Data`, `Schema`, `Array`, `Chunk`, `HashSet`, `HashMap`, `Match`, `Context`, `Layer`, `Config`, `Service`, `Record`, `Result`, `Exit`, `Bun`, `Platform`, `Command` and `ManagedRuntime`.
- Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, `switch`, `Object.values`, `Object.entries`, ad hoc `_tag` objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
- Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
- Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.
- Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Do not extract tiny wrapper or mapper helpers for single-use `Effect.mapError`, `Effect.catchTag`, `Effect.catchAll` or `Effect.catchAllDefect`.
- Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals.
- Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with `bun run changeset`, using a user-facing changelog summary. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.

Verification and handoff:

- Run this task's mandatory verification gates, including `bun run verification` unless the task explicitly documents a narrower gate.
- Report the Changeset path and release-train impact, or report why no Changeset was required.
- Report changed files, verification commands, outcomes and residual risks.
- Do not start or delegate another task. The parent agent must review, audit, verify and explicitly accept this task before the next task begins.

Task-specific scope: create `packages/sdk/typescript` package metadata, tsconfigs, README, explicit export paths and placeholder source entrypoints only. Do not implement calculator execution yet.
outputs: package-boundary test or script path if added
mandatoryVerification: import-boundary check proving no SDK dependency on @whattax/http-api and no root AU import
completionCriteria: SDK package exists and is included by the Bun workspace glob.
Export map separates browser-safe, Effect-native and testing paths.
Root entrypoint does not import AU rule packages or server-only modules.
Import-boundary check exists or the task records the smallest explicit follow-up for SDK-002.
Parent agent reviewed the diff against the spec, task and architecture docs.
Parent agent verified the Changeset.

Task-specific scope: implement typed SDK calculation/module descriptor types and `whattax/effect` calculation execution by delegating to `PublicCalculatorService`. Do not add plain Promise facade or AU convenience clients yet.
outputs: package-boundary tests or scripts wired into verification
mandatoryVerification: import-boundary check proving SDK has no @whattax/http-api source imports
completionCriteria: Descriptor types bind calculator id, jurisdiction, tax year, input schema and output schema.
Effect facade delegates runtime execution to `PublicCalculatorService`.
Type tests prove unsupported calculations and mismatched inputs fail at compile time.
Runtime tests prove Effect facade parity with `PublicCalculatorService` for success and guided errors.
Import-boundary checks prove SDK source does not import `@whattax/http-api`.
Parent agent verified canonical schema/type/id reuse.

Task-specific scope: implement browser-safe plain `WhatTax` facade, Data/Schema-owned safe results, documented SDK errors and `whattax/au` typed modules for current AU calculators. Keep convenience helpers thin and prove they preserve the generic descriptor model.
mandatoryVerification: type-level test command for plain facade and AU subpath misuse cases
completionCriteria: `import { WhatTax } from "whattax"` exposes plain Promise methods without Effect runtime types.
`import { au } from "whattax/au"` exposes typed current AU modules and calculations.
`safe` methods return schema-owned or Data-owned result values.
Type tests prove incompatible tax years and wrong calculation inputs fail at compile time.
Browser-safe entrypoints do not import server-only modules or `@whattax/http-api`.

tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.

- Read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in `docs/architecture/*`.

- MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: `Data`, `Schema`, `Array`, `Chunk`, `HashSet`, `HashMap`, `Match`, `Context`, `Layer`, `Config`, `Service`, `Record`, `Result`, `Exit`, `Bun`, `Platform`, `Command` and `ManagedRuntime`.

Task-specific scope: update `@whattax/http-api` handlers and route schemas to consume SDK-owned calculation facades/schemas where appropriate. Do not add any dependency from the SDK package to `@whattax/http-api`. Keep typed HTTP clients owned by `@whattax/http-api`.
mandatoryVerification: import-boundary check proving dependency direction is SDK -> HTTP API
completionCriteria: `@whattax/http-api` imports SDK calculation facades/schemas where appropriate.
The SDK package has no dependency on `@whattax/http-api` in package metadata or source imports.
Typed HTTP clients remain owned by `@whattax/http-api`.
SDK README includes plain, Effect-native and AU examples without HTTP API dependency examples.
Parity tests compare SDK, `PublicCalculatorService` and HTTP API results for success and guided errors.
Import-boundary tests prove HTTP API may import SDK, but SDK does not import HTTP API.
Architecture docs describe HTTP API as a transport consumer of the SDK.

Task-specific scope: prepare but do not automatically publish unless explicitly requested. outputs: packed artifact or clean install verification evidence
mandatoryVerification: live package-name availability check immediately before release decision
local pack/install smoke test for SDK import paths
packed artifact import-boundary smoke test for root, AU, Effect, schemas and testing entrypoints

completionCriteria: Package name decision is recorded from live verification.

No publication action happened without explicit user approval.
Parent agent accepted the release-prep evidence.
